title: Why the SOXS Semiconductor Bear ETF Is Surging as Chip Stocks Sell Off
excerpt: A Korean brokerage downgrade lit a fuse under chip stocks Monday, sending a controversial leveraged ETF surging while NVIDIA, AMD, and Broadcom shed billions...
content_html:
David Beren

Sun, July 19, 2026 at 9:31 AM PDT 5 min read

Quick Read

  • SOXS jumped 9% Monday on a chip selloff but sits down 92% year-to-date, a victim of compounding decay inside its daily-reset leverage structure.

  • AMD and NVIDIA each dropped roughly 4% after a Korean brokerage downgraded SK Hynix on fixed-price HBM contract concerns, sparking broad chip profit-taking.

  • Don't wait: the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just revealed his top 10 AI stocks. See the full list FREE now.

The Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bear 3X Shares (NYSEARCA:SOXS) jumped 8.95% on Monday, closing at $4.45, as the largest names in the chip complex reversed sharply. SOXS is an inverse-leveraged product designed to deliver roughly three times the daily opposite move of its semiconductor benchmark, so a red day for chips is a green day for SOXS. Today qualified.

What Drove the Chip Selloff Today

The session's trigger came from Asia. According to reporting compiled by StockStory, a South Korean brokerage lowered its Q2 earnings forecast for SK Hynix, citing the memory maker's reliance on fixed-price high-bandwidth memory (HBM) contracts. That downgrade rippled through the memory chain and, by extension, the broader semiconductor sector. "A lowered Q2 earnings forecast for SK Hynix, driven by its reliance on fixed-price HBM contracts, also contributed to the selloff across memory stocks," the report noted, which then bled into logic and equipment names as investors reassessed the durability of AI capex.

Layered on top were renewed Middle East headlines. TradingView cited "escalating Middle East tensions" and noted that "Renewed Middle East tensions also pushed oil prices higher, encouraging a shift to safer assets." That risk-off tone hit the market's most crowded trade: AI-linked semiconductors. Profit-taking finished the job. Reporting flagged that shares of Marvell Technology, Vishay Intertechnology, and Allegro MicroSystems fell in afternoon trading due to profit-taking in the semiconductor sector, with equipment names like Lam Research (down 5.83%) and Texas Instruments (down 3.93%) among the day's laggards.

Don't wait: the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just revealed his top 10 AI stocks. See the full list FREE now.

On the SK Hynix paradox specifically: shares plunged in Korea despite a strong Nasdaq debut, and the reporting attributes that gap primarily to the HBM pricing/contract concern flagged by the local brokerage downgrade. This triggered a reassessment of the sector, which was already facing questions about the durability of AI capital spending.

content_html_zh:
大卫·贝伦

太平洋夏令时间 2026 年 7 月 19 日星期日上午 9:31 阅读 5 分钟

快速阅读

  • SOXS 周一因芯片抛售而上涨 9%，但今年迄今下跌 92%，这是其每日重置杠杆结构内复合衰退的受害者。
  • 在一家韩国券商因固定价格 HBM 合同问题而下调 SK 海力士评级后，AMD 和 NVIDIA 股价均下跌约 4%，引发了广泛的芯片获利了结。
  • 不要等待：2010 年给 NVIDIA 打电话的分析师刚刚透露了他最喜欢的 10 只人工智能股票。 立即免费查看完整列表。

Direxion 每日半导体熊市 3X 股 (NYSEARCA:SOXS) 周一上涨 8.95%，收于 4.45 美元，芯片行业中最大的公司股价大幅反转。 SOXS 是一种反向杠杆产品，旨在提供其半导体基准日相反走势的大约三倍，因此芯片的红色日对 SOXS 来说是绿色日。 今天合格了。

是什么推动了今天芯片的抛售

本次会议的触发因素来自亚洲。 根据 StockStory 编制的报告，一家韩国券商下调了 SK 海力士第二季度的盈利预测，理由是该存储器制造商依赖固定价格的高带宽存储器 (HBM) 合同。 此次降级波及整个存储链，进而波及更广泛的半导体行业。 报告指出，“由于对固定价格 HBM 合同的依赖，SK 海力士第二季度盈利预测下调，也导致了内存股的抛售。”随着投资者重新评估人工智能资本支出的持久性，这一预测随后渗透到逻辑和设备名称中。

最上面的是新的中东头条新闻。 TradingView提到“中东紧张局势不断升级”，并指出“中东紧张局势再度升级也推高了油价，鼓励人们转向更安全的资产。” 这种避险基调打击了市场上最拥挤的交易：与人工智能相关的半导体。 获利了结完成了工作。 报道指出，由于半导体行业的获利回吐，Marvell Technology、Vishay Intertechnology 和 Allegro MicroSystems 的股价在下午交易中下跌，其中 Lam Research（下跌 5.83%）和 Texas Instruments（下跌 3.93%）等设备股当天表现落后。

不要等待：2010 年给 NVIDIA 打电话的分析师刚刚透露了他最喜欢的 10 只人工智能股票。 立即免费查看完整列表。

特别是关于 SK 海力士悖论：尽管在纳斯达克首次亮相表现强劲，但韩国股价仍暴跌，报告将这种差距主要归因于当地券商降级所引发的 HBM 定价/合同担忧。 这引发了对该行业的重新评估，该行业已经面临着人工智能资本支出耐久性的问题。
